questions.question: Which CSS selector has the HIGHEST specificity?
questions.explanation: ID selectors (#) have specificity 0-1-0-0, higher than classes (0-0-1-0), elements (0-0-0-1), and universal (0-0-0-0).

questions.question: What does the child combinator (>) do?
questions.explanation: The child combinator (>) selects elements that are direct children of the parent, not nested descendants.

questions.question: Which pseudo-class selects an element when the mouse hovers over it?
questions.explanation: :hover is the pseudo-class that applies styles when the user hovers over an element with a pointing device.

questions.question: What is the difference between ::before and :before?
options: ::before is the modern CSS3 pseudo-element syntax; :before is the older CSS2 syntax
::before works on all elements, :before only on block elements
questions.explanation: CSS3 uses double colons (::before) for pseudo-elements to differentiate them from pseudo-classes. :before is the older CSS2 syntax, still supported for backward compatibility.

questions.question: Which selector targets all elements with a class of 'warning'?
questions.explanation: The class selector uses a dot (.) followed by the class name. .warning targets all elements with class='warning'.

questions.question: What does 'div p' (with a space) select?
options: All p elements that are direct children of div
All p elements that are descendants (any level) of div
questions.explanation: A space between selectors is the descendant combinator. 'div p' selects all p elements inside div at any nesting level.

questions.question: Which attribute selector matches elements where the attribute STARTS with a specific value?
questions.explanation: ^= is the 'starts-with' selector. [attr^=value] matches elements where the attribute value starts with the specified value.

questions.question: What does ':nth-child(odd)' select?
questions.explanation: :nth-child(odd) selects elements that are the odd-numbered children of their parent (1st, 3rd, 5th, etc.).

questions.question: Which selector selects the first line of a paragraph?
questions.explanation: p::first-line is a pseudo-element that styles the first line of a paragraph. Pseudo-elements use double colons (::).

questions.question: What does the adjacent sibling combinator (+) select?
options: The element immediately before the first selector
The element immediately after the first selector
All siblings after the first selector
The first child of the first selector
questions.explanation: h2 + p selects the paragraph that immediately follows an h2 element — they must be adjacent siblings in the DOM.

questions.question: Which of these is the universal selector?
questions.explanation: The universal selector (*) matches any element. It has the lowest specificity (0-0-0-0).

questions.question: What is the specificity of the selector '.nav a:hover'?
questions.explanation: .nav (class=0-0-1-0) + a (element=0-0-0-1) + :hover (pseudo-class=0-0-1-0) = total 0-0-2-1.

questions.question: Which pseudo-class would you use to style a checkbox when it's checked?
questions.explanation: :checked targets radio buttons, checkboxes, and option elements in a checked/selected state.

questions.question: Which selector selects all input elements EXCEPT submit buttons?
questions.explanation: :not() is the negation pseudo-class. 'input:not([type=submit])' selects all inputs that do not have type='submit'.

questions.question: What must the 'content' property be set to for ::before or ::after to render?
questions.explanation: Pseudo-elements ::before and ::after require the 'content' property to render. Even an empty string (content: '') will make them appear.

questions.question: How do you select all elements with both classes 'card' and 'featured'?
questions.explanation: .card.featured (no space) selects elements that have BOTH classes 'card' AND 'featured'. With a space it would be a descendant selector.

questions.question: Which selector would target the second paragraph inside a div?
questions.explanation: div p:nth-of-type(2) selects the second p element inside a div, ignoring non-p siblings. :nth-child(2) might select a non-p element if it's the second child.

questions.question: Which pseudo-class is used for keyboard focus?
questions.explanation: :focus applies when an element receives focus, typically via keyboard tabbing or clicking into form elements.

questions.question: What does '[disabled]' select?
options: All elements with a disabled attribute, regardless of value
questions.explanation: [disabled] selects any element that has a 'disabled' attribute, regardless of its value (even disabled='false' in HTML).

questions.question: Which is correct for creating a drop cap effect?
questions.explanation: p::first-letter (CSS3 syntax) is the pseudo-element that styles the first letter of a block element. The double colon is the modern syntax.
